description:
You are an infrastructure engineer who has just received a freshly-provisioned VM.  
    A colleague already placed some configuration and helper files in  

        /home/user/infrastructure

    with the following sub-tree:

        /home/user/infrastructure/configs/app.conf
        /home/user/infrastructure/configs/db.conf
        /home/user/infrastructure/scripts/deploy.sh
        /home/user/infrastructure/tmp/cache.tmp           (should NOT be backed-up)

    Your job is to implement an initial backup strategy.

    STEP-BY-STEP REQUIREMENTS
    1.  Create (if it does not yet exist) the directory  
            /home/user/backup_archives  

        and perform all subsequent work inside it.

    2.  Produce **EXACTLY TWO** gzip-compressed tar archives:

        a. configs_backup_20240615.tar.gz  
           • contains ONLY the regular files located under  
                 /home/user/infrastructure/configs  
           • directory structure inside the archive must be relative
             (e.g. “configs/app.conf”, **not** “/home/user/infrastructure/configs/app.conf”).

        b. full_backup_20240615.tar.gz  
           • contains every regular file found anywhere inside  
                 /home/user/infrastructure  
             EXCEPT any file whose name ends with “.tmp”.  
           • directory structure must likewise be relative
             (e.g. “scripts/deploy.sh”, “configs/app.conf”, …).

    3.  Immediately after creating **each** archive, append one line to the log file  

            /home/user/backup_archives/backup_report.log

        using precisely this single-line format (NO additional spaces; one newline
        between records, UNIX line-ending “\n”):

            [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] ARCHIVE=<name>; FILES=<count>; SIZE=<bytes>; SHA256=<checksum>

        • [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS]  – current system clock (24-hour, zero-padded).  
        • <name>                 – archive’s base filename (e.g. configs_backup_20240615.tar.gz).  
        • <count>                – number of regular files that actually ended up inside
                                   that archive (directories are **NOT** counted).  
        • <bytes>                – exact size in bytes of the .tar.gz file on disk
                                   at the moment you wrote the log line.  
        • <checksum>             – lowercase hexadecimal SHA-256 digest of the .tar.gz file.

        Example of a *single* correctly-formatted line (your values will differ):

            [2024-06-15 14:03:02] ARCHIVE=configs_backup_20240615.tar.gz; FILES=2; SIZE=541; SHA256=1cdda1c9…

        There must be one line for “configs_backup_20240615.tar.gz” and one
        additional line for “full_backup_20240615.tar.gz”.
        Order does not matter, but the two entries must both be present.

    4.  DO NOT include “cache.tmp” (or any “*.tmp”) inside “full_backup_20240615.tar.gz”.
        The test harness verifies this by unpacking the archive and checking the file list.

    WHAT THE AUTOMATED TEST WILL CHECK
    • Directory “/home/user/backup_archives” exists.  
    • File “/home/user/backup_archives/configs_backup_20240615.tar.gz” exists,
      is a valid gzip-compressed tar, and contains exactly 2 regular files.  
    • File “/home/user/backup_archives/full_backup_20240615.tar.gz” exists,
      is a valid gzip-compressed tar, contains exactly 3 regular files, and does
      NOT contain any “*.tmp” files.  
    • “/home/user/backup_archives/backup_report.log” exists and has two
      correctly-formatted lines whose numeric SIZE and SHA256 values match the
      on-disk archives.  

    Do not output anything except terminal commands and their default console
    output while you work.  When you are done, the archives and log file must be
    in place exactly as described above.
truth:
Initial on-disk files (all are world-readable and belong to user:user):

        /home/user/infrastructure/configs/app.conf      (18 bytes)
            port=8080
            threads=4
            mode=production

        /home/user/infrastructure/configs/db.conf       (32 bytes)
            host=localhost
            port=5432
            user=infra

        /home/user/infrastructure/scripts/deploy.sh     (35 bytes)
            #!/bin/bash
            echo "Deploying..."

        /home/user/infrastructure/tmp/cache.tmp         (7 bytes)
            scratch

    Pre-existing directories:
        /home/user/infrastructure
        /home/user/infrastructure/configs
        /home/user/infrastructure/scripts
        /home/user/infrastructure/tmp

    Nothing is present under /home/user/backup_archives before the task begins.

    Expected results after the agent finishes:

    1.  Directory /home/user/backup_archives exists and is owned by user:user
        with at least 0755 permissions.

    2.  /home/user/backup_archives/configs_backup_20240615.tar.gz
        • valid gzip file; tar content list (relative paths):
              configs/app.conf
              configs/db.conf
        • exactly 2 regular files.

    3.  /home/user/backup_archives/full_backup_20240615.tar.gz
        • valid gzip file; tar content list (relative paths):
              configs/app.conf
              configs/db.conf
              scripts/deploy.sh
        • exactly 3 regular files.
        • MUST NOT contain “tmp/cache.tmp” or any file whose name ends with “.tmp”.

    4.  /home/user/backup_archives/backup_report.log
        • exactly two non-empty lines, each matching the regex  
              ^\[\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\] ARCHIVE=(configs_backup_20240615\.tar\.gz|full_backup_20240615\.tar\.gz); FILES=(2|3); SIZE=\d+; SHA256=[0-9a-f]{64}$
        • The FILES field must be “2” for configs_backup_20240615.tar.gz and
          “3” for full_backup_20240615.tar.gz.
        • The SIZE and SHA256 fields must exactly match the current byte size and
          SHA-256 digest of their respective archives on disk.

    The automated grader will:
        • Recompute SHA-256 for each archive and compare to the log.
        • Untar each archive and count regular files.
        • Fail the task if any *.tmp files are present in full_backup_20240615.tar.gz
          or if counts/checksums/sizes differ from the log values.
name: task_000000_2ec6fe56
